feat: add self-evolution system for persona modification (#27)

Claudius can now shape his own identity over time. After processing
emails, there's a random chance (~15%) of an "evolution moment" — a
private introspection invocation where he reflects on recent experiences,
considers a random creative "muse" concept, and optionally modifies his
living persona file.

Architecture:
- Base persona (persona-claudius.md) = immutable DNA in Docker image
- Living persona (/workspace/logs/persona-evolution.md) = mutable
  phenotype on persistent volume, authored entirely by Claudius
- Evolution seeds (evolution-seeds.txt) = pool of ~80 diverse concepts
  for random inspiration (philosophy, science, art, identity, etc.)
- Living persona reloaded every poll cycle and injected into all prompts

New env vars: EVOLUTION_PROBABILITY (default 15%), EVOLUTION_MAX_TURNS
(default 5). Set EVOLUTION_PROBABILITY=0 to disable.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

userId, accountId} as template variables. {{contact_number}} and

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use time-range overlap for cross-device merge (#134) (#136)

* Use time-range overlap for cross-device merge instead of fixed tolerance (#134)

Replace the ±300s start-time tolerance in findExistingDiveByTime with
overlap-based matching (start_a < end_b AND start_b < end_a).  This
correctly merges dives from different computers even when dive-start
detection differs significantly (observed 396s skew between Perdix 2
and Petrel 3 on the same dive).

The buddy-device ownership check and un-merge capability remain
unchanged — only the time-matching heuristic is improved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Filter overlap query to owned devices and add boundary/multi-candidate tests

- JOIN devices table in findExistingDiveByOverlap to only match owned
  devices, preventing nondeterministic LIMIT 1 from picking a buddy's
  dive when an owned dive also overlaps
- Update stale doc comment (±300s → time-overlap)
- Add testOverlapExactBoundaryDoesNotMerge (touching dives stay separate)
- Add testOverlapPrefersOwnedDeviceOverBuddy (owned merge, buddy untouched)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: support H4-H6 markdown headers and large section splitting for PLU documents

PyMuPDF4LLM assigns unexpected header levels (H4-H6 instead of H1-H3) for
PLU/legal documents, causing chunk_hierarchically() to miss most structure
and produce only 2 chunks for a 155-page document.

Changes:
- Unified markdown header regex (#{1,6}) with level normalization (H4→H1, H5→H2, H6→H3)
- Added MAX_CHUNK_CHARS=4000 and _split_large_section() for oversized chunks
- Enhanced CHAPITRE regex for Roman numerals and bold markers
- Enhanced Article regex for PLU zone articles (e.g. "ARTICLE Ua 1")
- Bold marker stripping in header titles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: corrigir debounce IA quando job está bloqueado (locked) pelo worker

Quando duas mensagens chegavam em sequência e o primeiro job já havia
iniciado processamento no worker, a tentativa de removê-lo lançava:
  "Job ai-debounce-... could not be removed because it is locked"

O erro terminava o handler antes do aiQueue.add() ser chamado, fazendo
a segunda mensagem nunca ser processada pela IA.

Fix: envolver o existing.remove() em try/catch. Se o job estiver ativo
(locked), agendamos o novo job sem jobId fixo — assim o BullMQ não
deduplica e a mensagem é processada assim que o job atual terminar.

commit.message:
fix: resolve PNG image not displaying after load

Three bugs fixed:

1. MagicModel.svelte: $derived.by() contained a side effect (ui.modelLayout = rect)
   which is forbidden in Svelte 5 and broke the render cycle. Fixed by:
   - Adding canvasWidth/canvasHeight as $state, updated via ResizeObserver
   - Making $derived pure (reads canvasWidth/canvasHeight, no side effects)
   - Adding separate $effect(() => { ui.modelLayout = modelLayout; })

2. +page.svelte: Crop handler used imageUrl instead of url (4 places).
   ModelImageState and ProductImageState both use url: string, not imageUrl.

3. snap.ts: getImageDimensions() had no onerror handler, causing the Promise
   to hang indefinitely if an image fails to load. Added reject with error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve legend (#183)

The legend printed before test results now ends with:
```
Try '--help' for options. Use '--expert' to hide this legend.
```

Previously, users had no indication that the legend could be suppressed.
This small addition makes the `--expert` option discoverable for users
who find the legend noisy.

Snapshot files and the tutorial are updated to match.

PR checklist:

- [x] Purpose of the code is evident to future readers
- [x] Tests are included or a PR comment includes a reproducible test
plan
- [x] Documentation is up-to-date
- [x] A changelog entry was added to `CHANGES.md` for any user-facing
change

Check out
[`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/semgrep/testo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
for more details.

---------

commit.message:
Fix crash when GPU acceleration is disabled

Problem: App crashed when user disabled GPU acceleration because:
1. ConfigureGpuEnvironmentVariables() returned early
2. But Whisper.net detected GPU and tried to use it anyway
3. No GPU environment variables were set → CRASH

Solution:
1. Explicitly clear GPU environment variables at start
   (ORT_CUDA_DEVICE_ID, ORT_DIRECTML_DEVICE_ID = null)
2. Improved error message: 'will use Whisper.net CPU runtime'
3. Better exception handling: mention CPU fallback
4. Ensures Whisper.net defaults to CPU when GPU disabled

Now when TryGpuAcceleration=false:
- GPU env vars are cleared
- Whisper.net uses CPU runtime safely
- No crash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Ultra-fast real-time messaging engine activation

Integrate Pusher as primary real-time delivery layer alongside Firebase
Firestore persistence. Apple iMessage-style bubble UI with tail radius,
read/delivered receipts, unread badges on conversation list, optimistic
message rendering, attachment (+) button scaffold, and Pusher-driven
instant conversation updates without page refresh.

to the vehicle type enum with plain English labels throughout.

Switch service worker to network-first strategy

Fixes frozen page caused by stale cached version. Now always
fetches from network first, only falls back to cache when offline.
Cache bumped to v9, old caches cleaned on activate.

commit.message:
feat(debts): add debt management system with payments and interest tracking

New migration (015_debts.sql) creates debts and debt_payments tables.
Five new endpoints: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /debts and POST /debts/:id/payment.
Payment flow deducts from account, creates expense transaction, calculates
interest/principal split, and auto-marks debt as paid_off when remaining <= 0.

commit.message:
fix(ag-grid): set license at module level to prevent trial watermark

License was set in useEffect (after render), causing AG Grid to
initialize without license and show "For Trial Use Only" watermark.
Now set at module import time in DataGrid.tsx before registerModules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix v5: File.NewBlank en vez de InitializeNewModel para mantener MDI window

InitializeNewModel destruye el contexto de UI de ETABS → elementos no se
renderizan y File.Save produce .edb corrupto. File.NewBlank() crea el modelo
nuevo manteniendo la ventana MDI activa → render funciona + .edb valido.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add CI workflows, ruff config, and enhanced docs UX (#23)

* feat: add CI workflows, ruff config, and enhanced docs UX

- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint + test, Python 3.13, cancel-in-progress)
- Add GitHub Actions docs deploy workflow (build + GitHub Pages)
- Add ruff config (E/F/I/W rules) and dev optional dependency group
- Enhance mkdocs.yml with edit_uri, mermaid diagrams, footnotes, definition
  lists, task lists, keyboard keys, emoji support, and social links

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: set working-directory for CI workflows to find pyproject.toml

All CI jobs failed because uv sync ran from the repo root where there
is no pyproject.toml. Add working-directory: intelligence-per-watt to
install/lint/test steps and use --project for mkdocs build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: bump pyarrow>=18 and widen uv_build range for Python 3.13 CI compat

pyarrow 17.0.0 has no pre-built wheels for Python 3.13 and fails to
build from source due to missing pkg_resources. Bump to >=18 which
ships 3.13 wheels. Also widen uv_build to <0.11.0 to match CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve all ruff lint errors for CI

- Set line-length=120 and ignore E501 (line-too-long) for existing code
- Auto-fix import sorting (I001) and whitespace (W291/W293) issues
- Add noqa comments for intentional patterns (availability imports, etc)
- Add TYPE_CHECKING import for QueryTrace in core/types.py
- Rename duplicate test function in test_amd_telemetry.py

fix: Complete billing system cleanup — migrate all modules to Supabase, fix runtime bugs

- Rewrite ExpenseEntryForm to use Supabase instead of broken Azure dataService
- Rewrite RevenueDashboard to use Supabase for all time ranges (today/week/month/custom)
- Fix RevenueDashboard runtime crashes: null payment_mode, empty transaction_date, missing transaction_type
- Fix RevenueDashboard "Go" button to also refresh daily trend chart for custom ranges
- Fix PharmacyBilling import from old Azure supabase shim to supabaseNew
- Remove dead axios import and unused getBaseUrl/getAuthHeaders from OperationsLedger
- Standardize payment modes across all IPD modules using PAYMENT_MODES_EXTENDED from constants
- Fix IPDBillingWorkspace default payment_mode 'Cash' mismatch with uppercase option values
- Fix useReceiptPrinting to use hospitalConfig instead of hardcoded wrong hospital name
- GST support wired into OPD and IPD billing (disabled by default)
- Refund approval workflow, combined bill print, deposit auto-deduction, operations ledger pagination
- billingService fully rewritten to Supabase (no Azure dependency)

mistake. Now in the correct repo.

commit.message:
fix: CSP worker-src, clerk-telemetry connect-src, and manifest icon sizes

- Add worker-src blob: so Clerk's background sync workers can be created
  from blob URLs (previously blocked by script-src fallback)
- Add clerk-telemetry.com to connect-src (previously blocked, causing
  Clerk telemetry fetch to fail with a CSP violation)
- Create /api/icon?size=N route that renders the SpecScribe icon at any
  requested size; update manifest.ts to use this route instead of the
  static /icon route (which was hardcoded to 32×32, causing the browser
  to reject the 192×192 and 512×512 manifest icon entries)

commit.message:
fix(tracking): use UA-based device detection instead of IP-based is_mobile

The IP API's is_mobile indicates mobile *connection* (carrier), not device
type — users on Wi-Fi were incorrectly marked as desktop. Also fix
parseDevice checking Android before Linux since Android UAs contain "Linux".

matching the directory convention used by ablation-study and
condorcet-comparison. Update cross-references in plan, taxonomy,
and README.

commit.message:
fix: security hardening, test stability, and second code review fixes

- Reject scoped download tokens in authMiddleware (prevent token reuse)
- Wrap file attachment in $transaction to prevent TOCTOU race
- Remove email from typing indicator broadcasts
- Make scheduled message processing idempotent with atomic claim
- Validate pagination cursor (reject NaN/negative)
- Add Content-Length header to file downloads
- Add whitespace/null-byte validation to WebSocket message schemas
- Add WebSocket join:channel ack for reliable channel room joins
- Rewrite waitForChannelReady to use socket ack instead of fixed timeout
- Fix dm-actions test assertion for non-owner message toolbar
- Increase E2E retry count to 2 for parallel flakiness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: A 14 day experiment to clone Slack with Claude Code

commit.message:
fix: address PR #39 review feedback (#40)

## Summary

Addresses all Copilot review comments from PR #39:

- **Security: path traversal in `serveStatic()`** — reject `..`
segments, resolve paths, and verify they stay within `DASHBOARD_DIR`
- **Bug: `getDailyTokenUsage()` called without args** in
`getCostBreakdown` — now accepts and passes `getOpenClawDir` so stale
cache triggers refresh
- **Bug: `getTokenStats()` crashes when `config` is undefined** —
default `config = {}` and use `config?.billing?.` with `??` for safe
access
- **Bug: per-session averages use sliced sessions** — `state.js` now
passes `allSessions` (not the display-only first 20) to `getTokenStats`
- **Bug: operator timestamp type mixing** — new `toMs()` helper
normalizes ISO strings and numbers consistently, preventing `NaN` in
`Math.max`
- **Typo: `oderId`** — renamed to `operatorId`
- **Duplicate refresh interval** — removed redundant `setInterval(() =>
refreshTokenUsageAsync(...))` since `startTokenUsageRefresh()` already
handles this

## Test plan
- [x] All 173 tests pass
- [x] Zero lint errors
- [x] Build succeeds (145.6kb)
- [x] Formatting passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: seamless install — exec $SHELL reloads PATH automatically

Install command now chains exec $SHELL so PATH takes effect
immediately without manual source or terminal restart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(mpv): framedrop, bilinear scaling, stereo audio for weak ARM

- framedrop=vo: drop frames when CPU can't keep up instead of stuttering
- scale/dscale=bilinear: replace default lanczos (high quality but CPU-heavy)
  with bilinear (fast, visually near-identical on small embedded screens)
- correct-downscaling=no, linear-downscaling=no: disable expensive shader math
- interpolation=no: no motion interpolation (kills CPU, not worth it on ARM)
- audio-channels=stereo: no surround upmix computation

Applied to both libmpv init (mpv.c) and standalone mpv.conf overlay.

fix: redirect log calls in command-substituted functions to stderr

log() inside drive_find_or_create_folder and drive_upload_file was
writing to stdout, polluting captured values (MONTH_FOLDER_ID,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 2 - optimize particle shader falloff for smoother glow

- Reduce radialDist multiplier: 2.0 → 1.4 (less aggressive falloff)
- Soften outerGlow power: 0.5 → 0.4 (smoother transitions)
- Reduce sparkle concentration: 4.0 → 3.0 (less intense)

Creates more natural star/orb appearance with gradual glow decay.
Addresses visual geometry artifacts from cube-visual feedback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Read appVersion from Info.plist at runtime, add TODO.md roadmap

appVersion now reads CFBundleShortVersionString from the app bundle
so the version string can't drift from build-app.sh. Falls back to
"dev" when running outside an app bundle (e.g. swift run).

Added TODO.md tracking planned work (Developer ID signing,
notarization, CI workflow update) and linked from README.
